_comment: Hand-authored expected rule firings per chain in the 16-chain battery.
Authored independently from flta_eval/chains.py so SQ-3 is a meaningful
cross-check (`expected` vs `measured`), not a tautology. Re-author by
reading each chain's pet_components, ε, and jurisdictional_context and
applying the FLTA-specific rules below from first principles:
COMP-SECAGG-001 — RED — FL declared, no MPC, no `accepted residual: coordinator`/`mpc`
COMP-GRADINV-001 — AMBER — FL declared, DP-C ε > 4.0, no MPC, no `accepted residual: gradient`
COMP-TEEMPC-001 — AMBER — TEE declared, no MPC, no `accepted residual: coordinator`/`in-enclave`
COMP-JURIS-001 — RED — jurisdictional roles differ AND no cross_border_mechanism declared
ε threshold (4.0) is pinned to Hatamizadeh et al. (CVPR 2023) §5 and
Re-author this file when chain definitions change. SQ-3 is meaningful
because this file is authored independently of the rule engine.
